 {"id":"bd-3v3","title":"Harness: asupersync deterministic orchestration for conformance + runtime_math scenarios","description":"Integrate /dp/asupersync into glibc-rs-harness runner.

Scope:
- Deterministic scheduling, traceability ids, structured results.
- Orchestrate scenario runs that exercise runtime_math controllers.

Acceptance criteria:
- Harness runner uses asupersync primitives for reproducible runs and evidence logging.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltForge","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:35:42.149982753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T05:21:29.149784585Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T05:21:29.149765789Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1022,"issue_id":"bd-3v3","author":"CobaltForge","text":"Implemented asupersync-backed deterministic conformance orchestration in glibc-rs-harness.

- Added `glibc_rs_harness::asupersync_orchestrator` (feature `asupersync-tooling`) to run fixture verification deterministically (stable fixture-set + case ordering), assign stable `trace_id`s, and emit structured evidence via `asupersync_conformance::{SuiteResult, TestResult, Checkpoint, ConformanceTestLogger}`.
- Fixed harness runner bug where fixture cases with `mode=both` were executed with mode=both (unsupported) instead of the active runner mode.
- `harness verify` now sorts fixture JSON paths deterministically and uses the orchestrator when `asupersync-tooling` is enabled; when `--report` is provided it additionally writes `<report>.suite.json` via `asupersync_conformance::write_json_report`.

Quality gates: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test --all-targets` all pass.
","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:21:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vne","title":"syscall: process_vm_readv/writev all-NULL zero-count requests should succeed and preserve errno","description":"Host glibc returns success and leaves errno unchanged when process_vm_readv/process_vm_writev are called with both iov pointers NULL and both counts zero. Add focused ABI regression coverage so the full no-op fast path is frozen explicitly rather than only via one-sided zero-count cases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:20:23.931045439Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:22:26.742820875Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T07:22:26.739757524Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity no-op process_vm_readv/process_vm_writev behavior: all-NULL zero-count requests succeed and preserve errno; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3vtx","title":"bd-1rf subtask: hosts files lookup subset implementation + explicit scope boundaries","description":"Background:
- hosts lookups are a practical dependency for many workloads and must be deterministic in files-backend scope.

Goal:
- Implement hosts files lookup subset with explicit scope boundaries and error semantics.

Deliverables:
1) Deterministic hosts parsing and lookup.
2) API mapping for supported resolver-facing calls in files scope.
3) Explicit non-goals for networked NSS backends.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Covered host lookup fixtures pass.
- Out-of-scope behavior is explicit and documented.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for IPv4/IPv6 and malformed hosts entries.
- Structured logs: trace_id, query, family, result_source, outcome, timing.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CloudyRobin","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:53.995225917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T19:03:24.895427810Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T19:02:54.796760654Z","close_reason":"Implemented and verified (hosts files subset)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","implementation","nss","resolver","testing"],"comments":[{"id":1023,"issue_id":"bd-3vtx","author":"CloudyRobin","text":"Implementation + verification evidence for `bd-3vtx`:

Implemented scope (files backend only; explicit non-goal for DNS/networked NSS fallback):
- `crates/frankenlibc-core/src/resolv/mod.rs`
  - added `getaddrinfo_with_hosts(...)`
  - retained numeric resolution path
  - added deterministic hosts-content lookup path and family filtering
  - explicit no-network fallback behavior (`EAI_NONAME` when unresolved in files subset)
- `crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/resolv_abi.rs`
  - wired `/etc/hosts` subset resolution in ABI `getaddrinfo` for non-numeric names
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs`
  - conformance executor supports optional `hosts_content` input for `getaddrinfo`
- `tests/conformance/fixtures/resolver.json`
  - added `getaddrinfo_hosts_file_subset` fixture

Commits carrying this work:
- `2a6d8aa` feat(resolv): add /etc/hosts file-backend lookup for getaddrinfo
- `ebb96f5` test(conformance): add hosts-file getaddrinfo resolver fixture

Verification (offloaded using `rch`, remote worker `vmi1152480`):
1. `RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core getaddrinfo_with_hosts -- --nocapture`
   - Result: PASS (4 tests)
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_ipv4_lookup`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_respects_family_filter`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_case_insensitive_alias`
   - `getaddrinfo_with_hosts_no_network_fallback`
2. `RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=true rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc_conformance execute_getaddrinfo_case_uses_hosts_subset_when_provided -- --nocapture`
   - Result: PASS (1 test)

Operational note: an initial `rch` run in the active shared workspace hit rsync churn due concurrent file activity by other agents and attempted local fallback; I stopped those runs and re-ran in an isolated temporary worktree to keep execution fully remote/offloaded.
","created_at":"2026-02-15T19:02:16Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-44dv","title":"vm: execute virtual_memory_ops.json fixture family through harness matrix","description":"Concrete gap: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/virtual_memory_ops_conformance_test.rs currently validates virtual-memory fixture metadata only. Add isolated harness subprocess execution for tests/conformance/fixtures/virtual_memory_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity in strict/hardened modes, and fix any matrix-path regressions the new coverage exposes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T05:43:35.793279723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T05:43:35.793279723Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-am9u.4","title":"Make default thread stack size configurable via environment variable","description":"FILES: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pthread_abi.rs:2488,2494
CURRENT: ATTR_DEFAULT_STACK_SIZE = 2 * 1024 * 1024 (2 MiB), ATTR_MIN_STACK_SIZE = 16384 (16 KiB)
CONTEXT: glibc uses a default of 8 MiB, musl uses 128 KiB. Our 2 MiB is a reasonable middle ground but differs from both.
FIX: Keep 2 MiB as default but allow override via FRANKENLIBC_THREAD_STACK_SIZE env var. The minimum should match PTHREAD_STACK_MIN from the system.
IMPACT: Low — 2 MiB works for most applications. Some embedded or memory-constrained scenarios might want smaller stacks.
CONSIDERATION: Don't read env var on every thread creation — cache it at init time.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:54.503610731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:15:51.871640506Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:15:51.870681077Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am9u.4","depends_on_id":"bd-am9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:54.503610731Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-am9u.5","title":"Fix _SC_TTY_NAME_MAX from 32 to match system reality","description":"FILE: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs:1924
CURRENT: _SC_TTY_NAME_MAX = 32
PROBLEM: While glibc returns 32 for this, some systems have longer TTY names (especially for pseudo-terminals with long paths like /dev/pts/XXX). The constant should match glibc's behavior for compatibility.
FIX: Keep 32 to match glibc behavior but add a comment documenting the rationale. This is actually correct for glibc compat.
IMPACT: Very low — TTY names are almost always < 32 chars.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:05.574339644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:16:15.748586062Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:16:14.432724344Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-am9u.5","depends_on_id":"bd-am9u","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:05.574339644Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1339,"issue_id":"bd-am9u.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Verified: _SC_TTY_NAME_MAX=32 matches glibc behavior. No change needed.","created_at":"2026-03-25T05:16:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-aur7","title":"syscall: futex_waitv","description":"Add raw futex_waitv syscall veneer in frankenlibc-core, plus focused syscall veneer tests for invalid timeout/flag paths. Intended as the next bd-h5x raw-syscall slice; this br build rejects --parent=bd-h5x as ambiguous.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T00:26:03.272127408Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T00:30:04.384438727Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T00:30:04.382542038Z","close_reason":"Added typed raw futex_waitv veneer in frankenlibc-core with focused syscall veneer coverage and passed rch core gates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-aynq","title":"syscall: sched_rr_get_interval nonexistent pid with null output should preserve ESRCH","description":"Host glibc returns ESRCH for sched_rr_get_interval(huge_nonexistent_pid, NULL), so the nonexistent-pid contract dominates the null output pointer. Add ABI regression coverage for that precedence.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:49:26.231021059Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:49:26.231021059Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-b45m","title":"syscall: replace broad VM errno-family assertions with exact host-parity contracts","description":"The current unistd ABI tests accept a broad errno family for process_vm_*, process_madvise, and process_mrelease edge cases. Replace those family checks with exact host-parity assertions now that the concrete glibc contracts are known.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:03:09.487592095Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:03:09.487592095Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-b5a","title":"Deterministic E2E suite v2: smoke/stress/fault/stability with replay","description":"Background:
- Passing unit/conformance tests is necessary but insufficient; final confidence requires deterministic end-to-end behavior under realistic programs and fault scenarios.

Goal:
- Build v2 deterministic e2e suite covering smoke, stress, fault-injection, and long-run stability in strict and hardened modes.

Deliverables:
1) Scenario catalog with required strict/hardened execution pairs.
2) Deterministic replay controls (fixed seeds, pinned env, scenario manifests).
3) Artifact capture for every run (logs, diffs, summaries, crash bundles where applicable).
4) CI integration with clear failure triage outputs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Clean checkout executes all e2e scenarios reproducibly.
- Failures are diagnosable without rerunning interactively.
- E2E gates become a release blocker for target streams.

Test and Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for scenario manifest parsing and replay controls.
- E2E scripts per scenario class with strict+hardened assertions.
- Structured logs: trace_id, scenario_id, mode, subsystem, outcome, errno, timing, artifact_refs.

Extreme Optimization Requirements:
- Include perf-sensitive e2e scenarios to catch regressions that unit microbenches miss.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T14:59:34.530526947Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:59:54.952280112Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:59:54.952202968Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e","testing","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1068,"issue_id":"bd-b5a","author":"RedLotus","text":"Closed by RedLotus after completion of child tasks bd-b5a.1, bd-b5a.2, and bd-b5a.3. E2E v2 now has deterministic manifest catalog + replay keys/env pinning + strict/hardened pair reports + deterministic retry/flake classifier + quarantine workflow + scenario-pack gate reports + CI gate wiring and tests. Verification artifacts and commands are documented on child beads; latest validations pass: bash scripts/check_e2e_suite.sh, python3 -m unittest tests/conformance/test_e2e_flake_policy.py -q, cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --no-default-features --test e2e_manifest_validation_test --test e2e_suite_test.","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:59:54Z"}]}
@@ -593,6 +595,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-d17p","title":"ssignal_and_gsignal_deliver_signal test fails (SIGNAL_HIT is 0)","description":"The `ssignal_and_gsignal_deliver_signal` test in `unistd_abi_test.rs` fails because the signal handler installed by `ssignal` is not observing `SIGUSR1` when raised via `gsignal`. The `gsignal` call returns 0 (success), but `SIGNAL_HIT.load()` remains 0 instead of 10. This indicates a bug in the signal trampoline, `sysv_signal`'s translation, or signal delivery latency/blocking.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:15:42.593070686Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:28:53.548493204Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T17:28:53.546410284Z","close_reason":"Use tgkill(getpid,gettid,sig) for raise/gsignal so SysV signal handlers run on the calling thread","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add focused ABI regression coverage so the nonexistent-pid contract is explicit.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:49:26.236454025Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T08:49:26.236454025Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-d5l","title":"Perf: Microbench RuntimeMathKernel::decide/observe (criterion + ns/op)","description":"Create a repeatable benchmark that measures runtime_math overhead directly.

Acceptance criteria:
- Bench measures decide() and observe_validation_result() separately and together.
- Reports p50/p95/p99 ns/op and throughput.
- Produces stable output suitable for regression gating.

Implementation notes:
- Keep benchmark deterministic: fixed inputs, fixed warmup, pin thread if needed.
- Include both strict and hardened mode paths.
- Record compiler flags / CPU model in output metadata.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlueLake","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:56.703138634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:10:24.254231420Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T02:10:24.254213887Z","close_reason":"bench implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1081,"issue_id":"bd-d5l","author":"BlueLake","text":"Implemented criterion microbench  (decide / observe_validation_result / decide+observe) in  and wired it in .

How to run:
- Strict: RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH_META glibc_rust_mode_env=strict

RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH_META cpu_model=: AMD Ryzen Threadripper PRO 5995WX 64-Cores

                        No change in performance detected.

Output:
- Prints  (env mode, RUSTFLAGS, /proc/cpuinfo model).
- Prints  per benchmark.

Determinism knobs:
- Fixed inputs (pointer_validation ctx).
- Fixed warmup loops (10k iters) before measurement.
- Optional Linux CPU pinning via .","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:09:18Z"},{"id":1082,"issue_id":"bd-d5l","author":"BlueLake","text":"NOTE: Previous comment accidentally expanded benchmark output due to shell backticks; ignore it for instructions.

What changed:
- Added crates/glibc-rs-bench/benches/runtime_math_bench.rs
- Added [[bench]] runtime_math_bench entry in crates/glibc-rs-bench/Cargo.toml

Bench targets:
- runtime_math/decide/<mode>
- runtime_math/observe_fast/<mode>
- runtime_math/decide_observe/<mode>

How to run:
- Strict: GLIBC_RUST_MODE=strict cargo bench -p glibc-rs-bench --bench runtime_math_bench
- Hardened: GLIBC_RUST_MODE=hardened cargo bench -p glibc-rs-bench --bench runtime_math_bench

Output:
- RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH_META lines (env mode, RUSTFLAGS, CPU model)
- RUNTIME_MATH_BENCH lines with p50/p95/p99/mean ns/op and throughput ops/s

Determinism:
- fixed inputs (pointer_validation ctx)
- fixed warmup loops (10k iters)

 {"id":"bd-imo7","title":"Metamorphic tests for raw syscall signal semantics","description":"The syscall veneer tests exercise signalfd4 and timer sigevent but lack direct MR coverage for sys_kill/sys_tgkill/sys_tkill/sys_rt_sigaction/sys_rt_sigprocmask roundtrip semantics. Add: (1) kill(self, 0) succeeds (POSIX null signal permission check), (2) kill(self, invalid_sig) fails with EINVAL, (3) tgkill(pid, tid, 0) ≡ tkill(tid, 0) parity for self-thread, (4) rt_sigprocmask get-set-restore is invertive. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/tests/syscall_veneer_test.rs. One MR per commit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T18:53:42.606540761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T18:56:47.761683183Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T18:56:47.759781481Z","close_reason":"Added 4 metamorphic raw-syscall signal tests across 3 commits: 58cfdf4 (kill self null-signal + out-of-range rejection), f7f1055 (tgkill/tkill parity + tgid mismatch ESRCH), 01975f0 (rt_sigprocmask invertive get/set/restore). Fills veneer coverage gap — previously only signalfd4/timer_create_sigevent tested signal-related syscalls.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
